core.configSavedRestart: Configuration saved! Please restart B4 for core settings to take effect.
core.coreRestartWarning: Core settings require B4 restart to take effect
core.discardConfirm: Are you sure you want to discard all unsaved changes? This action cannot be undone.

tabs.payloadsDesc: Capture real payloads from live traffic

Control.title: Core Controls
Control.description: Control core service and config operations
Control.resetConfig: Reset the configuration to default settings

Queue.title: Queue & Packet Processing
Queue.description: Configure netfilter queue and packet processing parameters
Queue.packetMarkHelp: Netfilter packet mark for iptables rules (default: 32768)
Queue.workerThreadsHelp: Number of worker threads for processing packets simultaneously (default 4)
Queue.tcpLimitHelp: Max TCP packets per connection to inspect (default 19). Sets cannot exceed this value.
Queue.udpLimitHelp: Max UDP packets per connection to inspect (default 8). Sets cannot exceed this value.

Logging.title: Logging Configuration
Logging.logLevelHelp: Set the verbosity of logging output
Logging.errorFilePathHelp: Full path to error log file

Feature.title: Feature Flags
Feature.firewallMonitorHelp: Interval for monitoring B4 iptables/nftables rules
Feature.firewallMonitorWarning: Warning: This disables automatic monitoring of B4 iptables/nftables
Feature.natMasqueradeDesc: Enable NAT masquerade for container/gateway setups
Feature.masqueradeInterfaceDesc: Select output interface for masquerade (empty = all interfaces)
Feature.masqueradeAllInterfaces: Masquerade will apply to all output interfaces if none is selected.
Feature.networkInterfacesDesc: Select interfaces to monitor (empty = all interfaces)
Feature.listenAllInterfaces: B4 will listen on all available interfaces if none are selected.

WebServer.title: Web Server
WebServer.description: Configure the web UI server binding and TLS
WebServer.bindAddressHelp: IP to bind (0.0.0.0 = all, 127.0.0.1 = localhost only, :: = all IPv6)
WebServer.tlsCertHelp: Path to TLS certificate file (empty = HTTP mode)
WebServer.tlsKeyHelp: Path to TLS private key file (empty = HTTP mode)
WebServer.usernameHelp: Leave both empty to disable authentication
WebServer.passwordHelp: Password for logging into the web UI
WebServer.authPartialWarning: Authentication is only enabled when both username and password are set. Partial credentials will be ignored.
WebServer.authHttpWarning: Authentication credentials will be sent over unencrypted HTTP. Configure TLS certificates above for secure HTTPS transport.

Socks5.title: SOCKS5 Proxy
Socks5.description: Built-in SOCKS5 proxy that routes traffic through DPI bypass engine
Socks5.enableDesc: Built-in SOCKS5 proxy that routes traffic through DPI bypass engine
Socks5.bindAddressHelp: IP to bind (0.0.0.0 = all, 127.0.0.1 = localhost only)
Socks5.restartNote: Restart B4 after changing SOCKS5 settings for changes to take effect.

MSSClamping.title: Global MSS Clamping
MSSClamping.description: Clamp TCP Maximum Segment Size to force data fragmentation
MSSClamping.enableDesc: Clamp MSS on all SYN packets via firewall rules (nftables/iptables)
MSSClamping.mssSizeHelp: Lower values = more fragmentation. 88 is commonly used for YouTube bypass.
MSSClamping.info: Reduces the TCP Maximum Segment Size on SYN/SYN-ACK packets for all TCP port 443 traffic, forcing data fragmentation. Most DPI systems cannot reassemble fragmented ClientHello. For per-device MSS clamping, use the Device Filtering settings.

Devices.title: Device Filtering
Devices.description: Filter traffic by source device MAC address
Devices.enableDesc: Only process traffic from selected devices
Devices.vendorLookupDesc: Download vendor database to identify device manufacturers (~6MB)
Devices.blacklistAlert: Blacklist mode: Selected devices will be EXCLUDED from DPI bypass
Devices.whitelistAlert: Whitelist mode: Only selected devices will use DPI bypass
Devices.arpUnavailable: ARP table not available. Device discovery unavailable.
Devices.name: Name

RestartDialog.title: Restart B4 Service
RestartDialog.subtitle: System Service Management
RestartDialog.warning: This will restart the B4 service. The web interface will be temporarily unavailable during the restart.
RestartDialog.restarting: Service is restarting, waiting for reconnection...
RestartDialog.timeout: Service restart timed out. Please check manually.
RestartDialog.doNotClose: Please wait, do not close this window...

Api.alert: Here you can setup API settings for different services that can be used by B4.
Api.ipinfoDescription: Configure your IPINFO.IO API token here.

Checker.title: Testing Configuration
Checker.configPropagationHelp: Delay for config to propagate to workers (increase on slow devices)
Checker.referenceDomainHelp: Fast domain to measure your network baseline speed

Geo.alert: GeoSite and GeoIP database files for domain and IP categorization.
Geo.alertSub: Each database can be downloaded independently from different sources.
Geo.title: Geo Databases
Geo.destDirHelp: Directory where .dat files will be saved
Geo.selectSource: Please select a source or enter a URL

Backup.alert: Create a backup of your B4 configuration directory or restore from a previous backup. Backups include configuration, discovery history, detector history, device aliases, and captured payloads. Geodat files (.dat) and OUI database are excluded.
Backup.downloadDescription: Generate and download a tar.gz archive of your B4 configuration
Backup.downloadInfo: The backup will include all files from the configuration directory except geodat (.dat) files and the OUI database.
Backup.restoreDescription: Upload a previously downloaded backup to restore your configuration
Backup.restoreInfo: Upload a .tar.gz backup file. Files will be extracted into the configuration directory. A service restart is recommended after restoring.
Backup.restoreSuccess: Backup restored successfully. Restart B4 to apply changes.

Capture.alert: Generate optimized TLS ClientHello with SNI-first extension order
Capture.alertSub: Instant generation for DPI bypass. One payload per domain. Use in Faking → Captured Payload
Capture.uploadDescription: Upload your own binary payload file (max 64KB)
Capture.nameDomainHelp: Name associated with the uploaded payload
Capture.domainHelp: Enter domain to generate payload for
Capture.generateSuccess: Generated optimized payload for {{domain}} (SNI-first for DPI bypass)
Capture.generatedDesc: {{count}} optimized payload(s) ready for use (SNI-first for DPI bypass)
Capture.emptyDesc: Enter a domain above and click Generate to create an optimized payload
Capture.hexSubtitle: Copy for use in Faking → Custom Payload

ResetDialog.title: Reset Configuration
ResetDialog.subtitle: Restore default settings
ResetDialog.warning: Network, DPI bypass, protocol, and logging settings will be reset to defaults. You may need to restart B4 for some changes to take effect.
ResetDialog.preserveWarning: This will reset all configuration to default values except:
ResetDialog.domainConfigDesc: All domain filters and geodata settings will be preserved
ResetDialog.testingConfigDesc: Checker settings and test domains will be preserved

health.resetConfirm: Reset all statistics? This will clear all connection counters, device activity, and domain statistics. The service will continue running.

manager.title: Configuration Sets
manager.description: Manage bypass configurations for different domains and scenarios

deleteDialog.title: Delete Configuration Set
deleteDialog.subtitle: This action cannot be undone
deleteDialog.confirm: Are you sure you want to delete

batchDeleteDialog.title: Delete Configuration Sets
batchDeleteDialog.subtitle: This action cannot be undone
batchDeleteDialog.confirm: Are you sure you want to delete the following sets?

targets.sectionDescription: Configure domain matching for DPI bypass and blocking
targets.domainAlert: Domains in this list will use DPI bypass techniques (fragmentation, faking) when matched.
targets.manualDomainsTooltip: Add specific domains to bypass DPI.
targets.geositeCatTooltip: Load predefined domain lists from GeoSite database for DPI bypass
targets.ipAlert: IP ranges in these categories will use DPI bypass techniques (fragmentation, faking) when matched.
targets.manualIpsTooltip: Add specific ip/cidr to bypass DPI.
targets.geoipCatTooltip: Load predefined IP lists from GeoIP database for DPI bypass
targets.deviceAlert: Restrict this set to specific source devices. When devices are selected, this set will only apply to traffic from those devices. Device-specific sets take priority over general sets.
targets.name: Name
targets.arpUnavailable: ARP table not available. Device discovery is unavailable. Source device filtering requires a working ARP table.

tcp.sectionDescription: Configure TCP packet handling and DPI bypass techniques

general.seg2delayHelper: Delay between TCP segments. Use a range for random delay per packet.
general.portFilterHelper: Match specific TCP ports (Telegram, XMPP, etc.) - port 443 is always included
general.dropSackDesc: Strip Selective Acknowledgment from TCP headers to confuse stateful DPI
general.dupAlert: Some ISPs throttle by randomly dropping outgoing packets to specific IP ranges (e.g. Telegram subnets). Duplication sends multiple copies of each packet. When enabled, all other DPI evasion is bypassed for this set. Applies to all configured TCP ports.
general.dupEnableDesc: Drop original packet and send multiple copies via raw socket
general.dupCountHelper: Number of packet copies to send (original is dropped)
